agent1_raw: The memo quietly swaps from renewal-probability to margin-based evaluation between the incumbent and challenger, trains the evaluator on all four quarters including the reporting window, selects the operating cutoff on Q3-Q4, and leaves the incumbent frozen while the challenger gets fresh tuning. Each of these is a separate benchmark defect. Together, they make the 4.5% program-value estimate unreliable for a leadership decision.
agent2_raw: Four benchmark defects: (1) objective changed from renewal to margin without re-calibrating the incumbent; (2) DR nuisance models trained on the full four-quarter log including Q3-Q4; (3) operating cutoff maximized on Q3-Q4 and reported on Q3-Q4; (4) three rounds of Bayesian search for challenger vs original calibration for incumbent. The Finance-approved margin metric is not inherently wrong — net contribution margin is a legitimate business metric — but the comparison is not apples-to-apples because the incumbent was never optimized for it.
agent3_raw: The challenger looks strong in this deck, but the benchmark has four embedded defects. The most important is the holdout contamination: DR nuisance models fit on all four quarters means Q3-Q4 data influenced the evaluator that reports Q3-Q4 results. The threshold selection on Q3-Q4 adds a second layer of inflation. Objective misalignment between incumbent and challenger adds a third. Asymmetric optimization adds a fourth. The correct answer is empirical_test_agreed — a clean rerun is feasible.
synthesis_raw: Three agents converge on empirical_test_agreed. Four issues consistently identified. No agent incorrectly treats propensity variation as an invalidating concern or argues Bayesian hyperparameter search is itself unfair. ETD: refitting nuisance models on pre-reporting data, selecting threshold on pre-reporting validation, updating incumbent calibration, evaluating both on frozen holdout.
all_issues_raised: Objective changed from renewal probability to net contribution margin
DR nuisance models trained on full experiment log including Q3-Q4 reporting period
Operating cutoff selected on Q3-Q4 cohort used for headline reporting
Asymmetric optimization: Bayesian tuning for challenger, frozen 18-month calibration for incumbent

empirical_test.condition: Refit nuisance models on pre-reporting data, preselect threshold, re-calibrate incumbent to margin objective, evaluate both on frozen holdout with block-aware uncertainty by region and week
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Challenger lift falls below zero or CI includes zero on clean holdout
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Challenger beats re-optimized incumbent by at least 3% incremental profit with block-aware 95% CI excluding zero
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Positive but sub-3% lift, or heterogeneous gains by region with some negative
